name: Buttondown
price_basis: one paid feature add-on, such as analytics or segmentation, checked 2026-07; core service is free through 100 subscribers
verdict_reason: TRIM: the first 100 subscribers are free. Kill the $9 add-on before you move the newsletter.
what_zo_builds: A small analytics and segmentation layer over exported Buttondown data. Zo can calculate cohort changes, tag subscribers, collect survey answers, and prepare reports without rebuilding a careful independent email service.
dont_kill_if: Your list is over the free tier or the add-on saves real editorial time. Buttondown is already cheap, independent, privacy-minded infrastructure. Do not spend six hours dodging a fair $9 bill.
build_brief: Goal: remove one Buttondown add-on while keeping Buttondown as the sender. Export subscribers and email events. Build a private /newsletter-report route that reproduces the exact analytics, tags, or survey view I pay for. Keep unsubscribe and sending inside Buttondown. Done when the replacement answers the three questions I actually use the add-on for and the newsletter still sends through Buttondown unchanged.
